title: T-026 — Partial-move split cascade
prompt:
# T-026 — Partial-move split cascade

PRD: `05-WAREHOUSE-PRD.md` §8.2 (FR-WH-011), V-WH-MOV-002.
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`.

## Goal
When `createStockMove` requests qty < LP.qty, run an automatic split (T-017) producing a child LP with the move qty, then move only that child. Source LP keeps remainder at original location.

## Implementation contract
`apps/web/lib/services/warehouse/stock-move-split-cascade.ts` exposes `splitCascadeMove(sourceLp, moveQty, destinationLocId, reason?)`. Steps: split (T-017) without optional move; create stock_moves on the new child LP only; insert lp_genealogy chain (split parent→child + implicit move relationship via context_jsonb).

## Files (scope)
- apps/web/lib/services/warehouse/stock-move-split-cascade.ts [create]
- apps/web/lib/services/warehouse/__tests__/stock-move-split-cascade.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given LP qty=100 at LOC-A and moveQty=40 to LOC-B, when splitCascadeMove runs, then a child LP qty=40 exists at LOC-B and source LP qty=60 stays at LOC-A.
2. Given the operation completes, when stock_moves is queried, then exactly one move references the child LP (no row on the parent).
3. Given the operation completes, when lp_genealogy is queried, then split row exists with operation_type='split' and quantity=40 plus context_jsonb.move_type='transfer'.

## Test strategy
- RED: stock-move-split-cascade.test.ts covers the three behaviors with fixture data.
- Run `pnpm --filter web test stock-move-split-cascade`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not generate a stock_moves row against the parent LP.
- Do not call T-017 sequentially with extra split (single split only).
- Do not bypass single-transaction guarantee.

pipeline_inputs.description: Partial move split cascade: split LP, then move only the child child to destination.
pipeline_inputs.details: Single transaction; one stock_moves row referencing the child LP; lp_genealogy split row with context_jsonb.move_type='transfer'.
acceptance_criteria: Given LP qty=100 at LOC-A and moveQty=40 to LOC-B, when run, then child LP qty=40 at LOC-B and source qty=60 at LOC-A
Given operation completes, when stock_moves is queried, then exactly one move references the child LP
Given operation completes, when lp_genealogy is queried, then split row exists with quantity=40 and context_jsonb.move_type='transfer'
test_strategy: RED: stock-move-split-cascade.test.ts asserts the three behaviors
risk_red_lines: Do not log a move on the parent LP
